name: Dwayne Burno
abstract: Dwayne Allen Burno (June 10, 1970 – December 28, 2013) was an American jazz bassist born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania who became a first-call musician on the New York City jazz scene.

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: An Evening of Irving Berlin and Cole Porter

works.t: Kiss Me, Kate: So In Love

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Sweet Lorraine: A Jazz Celebration for the Village Vanguard
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I had a quartet. When I came back – who did I have in that quartet? Kevin Hayes on piano, Dwayne Burno on bass, and Carl Allen, drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: Dwayne Burno will be on bass. And Donald Harrison – no. Donald’s making my summer tour on alto, but Don Braden’s going to play tenor with us for this Texas.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: He was in all the bands I did and their inception, my octet and my sextet, he was the guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022
